reviews.review_description:
"Narrative Choice" stuck bug  now seems to have been fixed by a silent update at least 5 months and possibly 11 months after release.    

The game still has its limitations but overall its fun if you like this sort of game and can live with a bit of Ui clunkiness and non standard choices (like using right stick for move).
1 year after release and only 2 updates (last update was 6 months ago). This VR version is only 30% of PC version and definitely not worth the money.
This is a really good game. Impressive graphics and interactivity. It would be nice to have a comprehensive tutorial / walk through round... and too bad there will be no more updates.
Please update the game. It's been two months since the "no update" update.

reviews.review_title: Could be a great game if not UX Designer
reviews.review_description:
That could be a really great step in CIV universe. But instead, mediocre UX and UI Designers spoiled the game. The only good Interface is at the startup, before you load the new world. After that - you never know what any option means, what any unit do, what city tiles are meant for. Half of the clicks are random and not meant to be, because you want to watch "i" information, and instead misclicked that small shaking button.
Fix the INTERFACE, make it more informative, like in old games. I dont need sloppy moving avatars of Leaders all the time, let them be only in Diplomacy Room. I need information about my Civilization, stable and comfortable. Make it fixed on the table, not shaking on the wrist.
Right now its only 20$ worth, not 60.

Updated review. For those who facing app crashes, there’s a hacky workaround. In your meta quest setting, go to general > storage, find the game and select clear app data. It should launch now without crashing but all your saved data will be gone. Still waiting for the fix..

reviews.review_title: Come on in. The water is fine.
reviews.review_description:
I like it! It’s civ in my living room on a giant table. It’s not perfect, but it’s civ, really! I was shy because of the early reviews but I’ve had no pressing issues. I wish it was ahhhh $40 or so, tho. But oh well sometimes you pay a tax for the privilege. I’ll come back to this again and again from time to time, guaranteed. 

I would choose this over a pc or console setup, now having tried it.

reviews.review_title: Crashilization never buy a Playside game
reviews.review_description:
Four months since release, new update.

There is still an issue where if you try to click on the quesionmark to get more info about what a critical and permanent choice does it will click the button for you instead. So game still not playable. :(

Two months have passed since the last update and not a word about any plans to fix this mess. I'm going to learn from this and never do the stupid mistake of buying a playside studio game.

They now released an update and the game still crashes when opening. Even more disappointed.


Like so many others I started to have the problem where the game does not even start any more. Why did they publish such a broken game?  Why has 2K started to publish junk? I have played every civ game since civ 1 with 4 colors and 320 x 200 graphics. Thousands of hours. I never expected to see a civilisation game on this shape. I really wanted to like it like all the other games on the series but this was rushed out far from complete.


The UI is extremely clumsy, i constantly have to move my arms in uncomfortable positions to read the text. There are placeholder strings instead of  names of objects. They have a huge button with  choices that can not be undone and then to figure out what the choice actually does you have to click on a very small question mark in the upper right corner. Problem is that it's very easy to accidentally click the big button instead and make a choice when you really wanted to know what your choices wre..

The game also only has one very repetitive song that plays over and over again which is very annoying.

Someone should feel pretty embarrassed for releasing a game in this state. It is not finished. Be aware that if you buy this game you purchase the right to be part of a beta test but as opposite to normal beta tests, there is no communication with the  developers on discord etc.

I wonder if they intend to fix these things at all it if they just dumped the game to cut their losses?

I played about 10 minutes. T
otal waste of money.
Can you please make it so you can grab the table to raise or lower it and maybe a tilt function would be great my wife and I play a lot of table top games in bed

I am 31 years old. This is the only game I play like twice a year. This VR version is the start of something special. I imagine this franchise doing amazing things in the updates and new versions to come. Playing this for 10 minutes made me feel good to be alive at a time where we can enjoy the things we love in full immersion. I don’t think it’s only going to be good for novelty. There is sometime really special here.

I want so much to enjoy this.  I want to pay $60 and I want to love this game as much as I love the flat version on my Xbox.  But this isn’t it.  It’s frustrating to move around, the graphics aren’t that good, the interface feels off, the yields look ugly as do a lot of the other in game assets.  It’s not even an issue that like the resolution isn’t good or the frame rate, it’s just ugly where it shouldn’t be.  The cities aren’t good looking, the units aren’t good looking, the map isn’t good looking.  Ugh.  I’m gutted because civ is my favorite franchise of all time.  I have thousands upon thousands of hours into this franchise, and my quest is my favorite way to play right now.  I’m afraid I’m going to request a refund and hope they can improve upon this, because it’s just not pleasurable…I have no desire whatsoever to continue playing this version.

Don't be put off by negative reviews a lot of that is to do with the terrible state the game was on launch day.

You are getting the full PC game here as far as gameplay goes not a cut down or simplified version. Yes the graphics are not as good as on PC but that is to be expected given the Quest's limitations.

It is considerably cheaper to by this version rather than the flat screen version and is worth every penny in my book.

The user interface could use a tweak or two but other than that this is a perfect VR port and I personally find it the most enjoyable way to play CIV 7.

DEVELOPERS: Please make the small information panels bigger so we don't have to scroll through them and make all info available on the panel at once so we don't have to click for more info.
Lastly you can pin one large panel off our choice to the wall on either the left or right side. Let us pin both one to the left side and one to the right side.

If Civilization VII keeps crashing at startup and there’s no support available yet, this workaround might help:

Steps:

1. Go to General Settings > Storage on your headset.


2. Locate Civilization VII in the list.


3. Tap the data icon (to the left of the uninstall bin) and delete the game data.



This will erase your save progress, but it should allow the game to launch without crashing.

Please upvote this post so more people can see it.

And 2K , please fix the game!
As a developer who’s been working on VR since 2016— as a huge fan of both VR and the Civilization franchise—I believe this game has real potential in VR.  It just needs some serious tweaking to deliver a smoother VR experience. I'm personally willing to help if needed.

The game had so much potential, but instead of redesigning the game for a VR experience it feels as though all they did was try to add a couple of neat scenes. It’s clunky at best, and at that high price point nothing but disappointed. ￼

3rd UPDATE

Game randomly fails when “narrative” happens. I’ve submitted video of this to support, I’ve explained that it happens, all the time, every combination, finally they asked me to provide every step leading upto the failure! Other than stating the above I ran out of patience. The game will not improve. Latest fix seems to fix nothing.

Back to 1 star.  


2nd UPDATE

Bought game again, first patch seems to stop gabe crashing. Raised rating to two stars.

Still lots of issues, promotions don’t work, game unstable again after 40 turns, not enough information of what units can do, tutorial very basic, difficult to see who is who; various zoom functions don’t work.  

Original review 

After a two sessions game just stopped playing?

Removed and reinstalled, gets to second start up screen and just gives up kicks me back into quest screens.

All software up to date for headset and game.

UPDATE

I have exhausted avenues with 2k the developer who claim it’s an issue with meta.

I have exhausted avenues with meta who claim it’s the developers issue. Who also claim no known issues with the game despite quite a few reviews who have same issue as me!!

After restarting the headset and every other possible thing to try and make the game work well nothing seems to fix the horrible stutters around the war table.

It looks and runs great everywhere else in the game, but once you get to the War Table it just falls apart. Not sure what the deal is but any time you move the map, look around the table it’s stuttering constantly. So guess I’m waiting for an update or a refund.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for your feedback and sorry you’re experiencing some performance issues. Please ensure your Meta Horizon OS firmware is up to date and download the most recent update for Civilization VII - VR that has just been deployed . This patch should help fix some of the performance issues you've experienced. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: Careful with spending your money on this
reviews.review_description: I got to play it for about 2hrs. I really enjoyed what was there, but now when I try to launch the game a 2nd time, it just crashes right after the intro screen.  Duplicated after restarting the headset and uninstalling, reinstalling the app. Can't refund due to playtime.  App is an expensive space of digital waste for me now.
Sucks i do not have a lot of the changes they have updated.too war prone

I love Civ, would gladly pay $60 for it. This game is close to being awesome, but issues make it unplayable. I tried so hard to ignore the issues, but now I’m for a refund. Will rebuy if patch notes include the following -easy- fixes. 

Notes from a senior UX designer:

1. Performance issues…like buttons not being press-able, I’m sure that will eventually be fixed.

2. Map nav. Why the hell do I have to point at the board to navigate? Just make the right stick always move the board. Bonus points if you let me use the grab button to move the board. Grab w/ both hands to pinch-zoom and rotate.

3. Table manipulation. Why the hell are there big blue dots, that if you click on, send the table all over the place? Make table manipulation an on-off switch. It’s a one-and-done workflow. This means on/off switch… Not continuous blue globes hogging much-needed performance strength.
When on, you use left and right sticks to adjust x, y, z, and size. Notice how I included “y”. There currently is no height control. The fug.

Hit me up if you want more free UX. I love Civ.
If you are looking for a big AAA game with sofisticated mechanics, with a good graphics and long gameplay then this is the game for you. Absolutely amazing! 5 stars!

reviews.review_title: If you actually play civ you’ll ❤️ it
reviews.review_description: This is by far the best game I’ve played on the meta quest 3. I’ve been playing civ for decades and tbh this is a dream come true; almost exactly what I imagined playing civilization in VR. There’s definitely a learning curve for those who are new to the game which is where I think all the low ratings are coming from. Sure it’s not perfect and there’s room for improvement but there’s no way this is less than a 4 ⭐️ game as is.

reviews.review_title: Fix it and I’ll fix my review
reviews.review_description:
Why even release it?? You have KNOWN about these issues (won’t even launch?! Buggy, crashes constantly if you can even launch it, horrific graphics, terrible rendering, and awful movement/leader interactions). Yet you pushed this out as a clear money grab instead of actually delivering what COULD BE the greatest AAA game on quest. Only reason I haven’t gotten a refund is because I got it 30% off with Quest+ and I can’t get that discount back now that it’s spent. PLEASE FIX THIS GAME !! 

It’s not even the game changes from past Civilizations that’s an issue. It is 100% Meta issues and 100% those who worked on the VR side. MAKE THE CHANGES BECAUSE I KNOW YOU CAN

I’m an avid civilization player.   This program is JUNK!  Do Not Purchase. It crashes may ways.  Then it will not restart.  If you delete data it will restart but then you have lost all your data and can’t restart your game.  JUNK!! jUNK!!!

I know the Civilization series tends to launch its games before they are finished, but overall, the base game is playable. This VR version is not. The base game is smaller than the original PC version, and, for some reason, they decided that crucial information can disappear or is not shown. For example, your city's information, name, and religion are missing. I need to convert my city; no ID information is missing. Does my city have a recourse slot free? No ID information is not shown. What does a specialist do on this tile? Sorry, information is not available. Oh, I can make a special person. What does he do? Info not available, and so on... So, my honest opinion is to wait another year or 2. maybe the product will be finished or at least playable.  Now you pay 60 euros for a beautiful table. After the new update, the basic game functions aren't working...

After playing the game for a day, it now consistently crashes on the load screen. Restarting the headset (Quest 3) and reinstalling the game has no effect. Even when it was playable yesterday, it was riddled with bugs that would require repeatedly reloading from a save point or restarting the game to get past. I would avoid this one.

This is an edit to my original 2 star review, which i gave due to table jitter that was making me motion sick. They fixed this with a day one patch, and now i am able to enjoy VR version of this game fully. Fun game, and an amazing accomplishment for a standalone game.

Further edit: I've played a few hours of the game and have changed my recent 4 star review to 5 star. IMO this is the best medium to play Civ 7 on. It really puts you *in* the game.

Briefly speaking, the game has potential but is marred by low FPS when looking at the main gameplay table and interacting with it. I tried to play for a while, but I know many people would have given up after a few minutes of this persistent stuttering. This makes the game unplayable at the moment. It needs a fix, and quickly. It should not have been released in this state. Beta testers? Play testers? QA? Hello? Premium price for a Quest game and falls at the first hurdle...not playable.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for your feedback. Please download the most recent update for Civilization VII - VR that has just been deployed, as that should alleviate some of the performance issues you've experienced. Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Massive disappointment from a long time fan of the series. 

There are so many bugs and issues that it makes a basic game unplayable. 

Inability to access key information is the biggest problem with some menus, icons and infographics simply not viewable. Issue only fixed momentarily by reloading the savefile. But in a game all about decisions, being forced to reload a save every time you have one to make is unmanageable. 

Lot of potential if they are to fix the bugs and overhaul the UI but I'm not very optimistic given the state of the PC version. 

Regret my purchase entirely. 

For a company of this size and for a price this high, the standard is unacceptable. 

Avoid.

I finally got the hang of it but then the game crashed. I tried to restart and reinstall but to no avail. All this on the Quest 3 and the Quest 3S. On the 3S it suddenly worked again only to crash seemingly permanently.  I read some other players have the same issue. It would be nice to get some information about how to fix it.

Townsmen is awesome! Both controls and gameplay. I expected a lot more from a Sid Meyer title, but it's worse by far. It's not finished yet. But nice try. I asked for a refund of this game but they refused. This is against EU Laws, but it's probably the only way they can make money on this game...

I am very frustrated with this game. It initially worked and although buggy I enjoyed a weekend afternoon of gameplay but then like a lot of the reviews currently the software just stopped working last night. I deleted the app and reinstalled and continues to not load. Because I have played this for more than two hours Meta will not give me a refund and now I am stuck with this POS that doesn’t work. This isn’t even beta software. it’s clearly not ready for GA or even a beta test and yet they went ahead and released it. ￼

reviews.review_title: Just ok with lot of issues
reviews.review_description:
Ive been playing Civ since Civ1, so when I first got into VR in 2019 I remember thinking how cool it would be to somehow play Civ in VR but thought it would never happen since the type of game Civ is. Fast forward to now and here we are, a dream come true for me. Yes there are graphical issues and its not as sharp as the flat version but thats what I expected since on a quest. Actually the game is sharper than batman so I would recommend to use quest games optimizer (google it if dont know, i used it with batman also) and set the cpu to very high and gpu to ultra, dont change the resolution as it wont make a  difference. There will be no more jitter and way less shimmering. There  are also some other smaller bugs but game is fun and very playable.Waiting on an update now though because after a few hours of playing now it crashes everytime i get to the loading take two screen for some reason even without using quest games optimizer or rebooting quest 3 doesnt help and i dont want to delete and reinstall it because ill lose my saved game. Please update this soon and thank you for making this! I will rate 5 stars when the crashing fix is updated. 

Update on 5-2-24: well now theres been an update i can actually play it without crashing at startup, after about 6 more hours of playing ive noticed quite a bit of other bugs, and the more i play (and the more other AI stuff is going on) ive noticed the graphics are significantly worse. Seems the game keeps changing the resolution to compensate for the load in processing. Ive lowered my rating down to 3 stars now since while its still playable, the fun has been sucked out of it since ive been in the exploration age, when everything is a blurry mess, cant promote army commanders, the legacy goals dont show as accomplished when you complete them in the lists(they once and a rare while will show), sometimes game wont let you move something (fix for that is reboot the game) which seems to happen the more i play, the level of the world in the table constantly changes either way too high or so low the ground doesnt even show (just a grid pattern), plus many more issues. These bugs are inexcusable at this point. I want the game to succeed in vr but ive decided to only play the flat version until i see multiple patches that address all these issues, the game just isnt fun past the first age and i can only imagine how much worse it is playing the 3rd last age as theres even more of a load running the game. Also not to mention the issues they have already fixed on the flat version dont get updated to the vr version at the same time(supposedly they will at some later date) so this will be on the shelf for awhile for me.

First I liked the game. But when I wanted to start it the next day, it crashes after the first screen. 2K may say, it is with Meta, but I don't have it with any other game. The company try to blame others. When you hear that the PC version is also packed with bugs, it seems clear to me. 60€ for a completely unplayable game is expensive.
A crash after the first load screen is extremely fast! If this is the path that 2K is taking, you can only hope for a failure of the company. Then they certainly can't swindle other players anymore.

Only played 1.5 hours so far but seems great. Playing on a quest 3s in mixed reality and dont seem to have any of the stuttering issues others mentioned

Edit: today the game wont even start, goes to black screen and just dumps me back into meta home screen. Will revise my rating when this is fixed.

Edit 2: delete app data seems to have fixed my issue, still crashes now and then. Still addicting game

Can’t find any better words to describe this than half-a**ed. 

I love Civ to death, and I spent thousands of hours on Civ 6, but this is just a mediocre, half-baked port. 

Nobody here is expecting stunning graphics or anything like that but Christ does it take that much to make a text fit into its box? How did they not get that right?

And the audacity to ask for $60 for this. Woah.

I was able to play this last two nights and looked forward to playing tonight but now the game crashes immediately after starting. Meta quest 3 is updated and I uninstalled and reinstalled civ vii. Other games work fine. Please fix this soon!!

reviews.review_title: A fix for those crashing at game opening
reviews.review_description:
This is less of a review but more a helpful hand to those crashing at the starting screen...Don't reinstall....

Go into your storage settings and look on the list for civilisation... Then -  clear the app data...
It should now work... 
Then restart the game and play in VR only
, not mixed reality... it seems to be an issue that causes the crash...    Hope that helps..

 occurs that can break the game in mixed reality mode...

Personally, I like the game so far... But it certainly needs some fixing... 
And where the hell is England ? Elizabeth, the 1st was one of my favourite leaders...

reviews.review_title: Great game don't listen to bad reviews
reviews.review_description: I have put several hours into this game, and I have not ran into any issues. If you have played civilization before, you shouldn't have much trouble with the controls though it does take some getting use to. Great game totally worth it. It seems they have fixed most issues that I've read from launch

BACK TO ONE STAR FOR ONE MONTH WITH NO UPDATES. FIX YOUR BROKEN GAME. WTF?

Update came out that made the game playable, now the downsides of the 'playable' game. 

the bad:
-Units don't render properly, they all have weird triangle heads that look like they belong in ps1 tomb raider. 

-Menus in civics, tech, etc bug out once in a while and stop showing the info boxes that describes upgrades, this requires a hard restart to fix (long load times make this annoying)

-when zooming out on the table, troops are replaced by chess pieces. When zooming into the table you get to see the cool detail of the world, downside is: there is no adjustable pan speed, to visit your other towns without zooming out, its painstakingly slow, you can click around the expanded map, but that's just clunky.

the good:
- this is the civ we all know and love with a new twist on it - VR. I look forward to future optimizations, and the possibility of this coming out on steam so everything doesn't look like a potato, because I can use my PC hardware, rather than the toaster on my head.
How could the developers do this to such a good franchise, I really wanted to like it, I really tried, but they have ruined the game

￼ I got the game the other day was playing yesterday morning. Then came home after work went to go play and it just kicks me out every time I try to play now I did an update that didn’t work. Try a few other things so I guess $60 down the drain. I knew I shouldn’t have spent that much.  ￼

I love these games, I have the last two on PC and had such high hopes for this game. I love this idea but the execution is lacking. I've had it for a week and it's crashed and I've had to completely wipe all progress in order for it to stop crashing after the launch page. After I got it working I've noticed that often the game glitches and in close up mode units will move and they'll leave a "ghost" version behind on the tile and won't go away. The tile becomes playable but looks like something is standing there. Also I've had lots of issues with general points, the screen to learn from the perk tree never shows on multiple different games. So the general points aren't usable which sucks. I hope they patch it soon and fix some of these bugs.

Great to see the care and thought that went into the game - the features that are brought into this help make it so immersive and definitely capture the “one more turn” that the flat screen games do.

It does feel like there could be some more polish added to the game - small bugs where zooming out and back in made units disappear and tutorial not quite catching up to the player actions make it feel like there is a need for an update/patch. If you love civilization and understand the mechanics it will be fairly quick to get the hang of.

Best tip: Read the tips/tutorials thoroughly!

reviews.review_title: max Disappointment cause not even starts
reviews.review_description: I've been playing Civilization since version 1 and have experienced many highs and lows throughout the series. I was full of hope for the Quest 3 version. Unfortunately, I'm more than just disappointed. So far, every game I’ve bought has run on the Quest 3 without any major issues. This is the first time that a game or application from the store has completely refused to start at all. I see the first loading screen, and then the game closes, taking me back to my virtual environment. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the game several times, restarted the Quest 3 multiple times—nothing helps. I'm beyond disappointed, especially since this was advertised as premium content.

a few still unpolished corners and minor performance issues (most hiccups gone after first patch) aside, it's a game with more breadth and depth of action and simulation than anything else on Quest

bugs are temporary, Civilization is forever. Let the barbaric shill tribes scream at my gates...

reviews.review_title: Sweet to have civ in VR, but oh so buggy
reviews.review_description:
Edit4
Tried MP with only AI again. Crash to desktop after 86 turns in ancient. Still not stable. Still no word from devs acknowledging problems and confirming fixes coming. So unprofessional for a $60 price. I just want to play without fundamental problems...

Edit3
Retried MP with only AI. Menu changes only occurred when i changed map setup. Seems default settings may be broken. I played for an hour until the game crashed to desktop. There's no way these problems wouldn't have been discovered by even the most innept QA and the game was launched at $60 price point anyway. I think the game itself will be excellent once it's actually fixed, but it should have been delayed and fixed prior to release. 

Edit2
I tried playing MP with my friend. Completely broken. Menu to "build" absolutely not showing up on mine or his at all. We tried reloading, recreating, messing around for over an hour. Nothing. I'm on v76 and he's on v74 so it's a fundamental problem. 

I then played a full default settings sesh in SP. That actually mostly ran fine. 5 hours to full completion. Had one bug instance where i almost got stuck. I had to apply a tile upgrade but it would keep reverting and asking to do again. I managed to skip turn. Lots of menus based triggering, text formatting, and menu all over the place issues but mostly serviceable. Main issue i had was actually the offhand control position. After about the 3rd hour of holding my wrist upright, my hand, wrist, and elbow started feeling it. By the end i was getting pain. So i think it would be good to not have the wrist rotate menu on the offhand and just use it for it's normal default control menu so people can keep it active by relax their wrist in a more normal and ergonomic orientation.

Civ 7 itself is the pros and cons of the games design itself. Ages are poorly implemented but i think the concept would be decent if the age transition was more natural and organic like in previous civ games. I like building out the city tiles proper. Not sure what all means what yet but much better handle on the flow after playing through a sesh.

All in all i really hope this game gets allot of fixings because it has potential to be a solid civ game in VR. I will continue to update my rating as the game gets fixed and improved. For now I'm upgrading it 1 star because i was finally able to run a full and fun sesh with minimal incident and could see where the game can shine.

Edit1
Encountered loads of issues with linking 2k account for MP and then crashes at startup after powering down the headset after v76. Remedied the crash by deleting app data for civ 7. Finally got account linking to work by removing 2 factor authentication and unlinking the meta account that i had already previously linked via PC.

Seriously, for a product asking a premium piece, it should be premium quality. Meaning bug free at launch. I'm down grading review for now until these bugs are cleaned up and the UI is actually serviceable in VR. Game has potential but geez this stuff is frustrating. I spent 4 hours just trying to set up account links just to play MP. Not the "one more turn" i was going for...

Original review.

It's awesome to have civilization in VR. No stuttering or motion sickness in my experience (which I'm prone to). Very immersive and diplomatic encounters have weight. Has the civ complexity and one more turn essence.

Here are some improvements that are absolutely needed. The UI needs some TLC. I'd advise studying demeo with grip buttons to move the table and navigate and zoom the board, not handles and trigger and analog sticks. Hold Trigger to pick up and move a unit would feel more natural. Menus need to not be neck craning and should all be center focus or attached to the table in segments. Seated mode should be considered a bit more. I struggled to get the table close enough to play seated but still had to reach up. Graphical updates to the PC versions would be welcome but these are definitely serviceable.

All in all I'm really enjoying this adaptation and i want to see it succeed. With the aforementioned improvements, this could be a shining gem in the meta quest library. Additionally adding the civ 7 improvements made since Feb would be most welcome. I'll update the star rating with improvements cause this has potential to be a 5 star once the VR portion is normalized to current VR control and interface standards.

Thank you for making this!

i was excited to try a new full price vr game as theyre not many "full" games available etc. I launched the game to play multiplayer (have no friends) i went to join a public game and the server list was straight empty. a while later i found one person on the list in an empty room but when i hit join it tells me we're on incompatible platforms. buggy edgy incomplete MARK

reviews.review_title: I enjoyed it until it stopped launching
reviews.review_description:
Bought the game on launch day and played for a few hours. Friday afternoon the game started crashing at black Civ 7 loading screen and hasn't worked since. Even a factory reset did not fix it.  
I was starting to enjoy it. Fix it!

When the game reached the 44th round, it started to crash! It could no longer proceed normally.

I unlocked an achievement in the game. What are the other achievements?! Where can I check if there are any other achievements?

Make the round table bigger. When it shrinks, the troops and buildings shouldn't turn into chess pieces, which makes the game less exciting. I clicked on the enemy's troops but didn't get any detailed information. The function of troop reinforcement has failed. As the game progresses, the detailed introductions of technologies and civics don't show up when clicked. Please fix it as soon as possible.
This game is a buggy mess. Just play flat mode

As someone who was really into CIV 5 and also love playing VR I am thoroughly impressed by the quality of this game so far. I read the reviews on day one and was very nervous about glitchiness and issues but decided to buy it today. After going through the basic tutorial the vr controls were really intuitive. The game play was pretty smooth and graphics were on par for other vr games. They seem to have done a big update and it’s working better than what those initial reviews said. I’m giving it a 4 because it was not 100% free of issues. I noticed that some of the screens didn’t work perfectly, especially after an hour and a half of playing. And the controls did start to get a little glitchy after that long as well. I think there’s room for some improvements with new updates but I can definitely recommend it. For how complicated of a game CIV is they did a really good job putting it into vr.

CIV 7 is definitely a very fun and interesting game to be in VR and I’ve overall had a pretty good experience playing, definitely pushes Quest 3/3S to its limits definitely in the battery area I recommend a battery strap, also I’ve experienced a few bugs, like the game having some buffering and the UI is not perfect but definitely isn’t horrible, I don’t recommend this for anyone who isn’t into strategy ￼games or just complex games in general because this definitely takes that margin, the game overall is still fun and enjoyable, I’ve not tried multiplayer yet but i plan on it, I hope we’ll see future patches and possibly get the DLC’s the main game has in future. Anyone who gave this 1 star in my opinion is overreacting and should look at the game in a different perspective, I think the worst rating currently is 3 stars

reviews.review_title: Looks & runs good, polished, full game
reviews.review_description:
Awesome experience thus far. Game looks good and runs good in both VR and MR mode (original launch day Quest3 512gb). Mechanics are polished, and there's a full game here. Yes you can zoom in and out on the map (something not shown in the previews). Happy with my purchase.  

Tip - for other games that have performance issues on launch, i find a headset reboot fixes it.

reviews.review_title: App crash FIXED, love the game
reviews.review_description:
Fixed Crash Issue-Paradise1990 provided a fix in a review: Steps: 1. Go to General Settings > Storage on your headset. 2. Locate Civilization VII in the list. 3. Tap the data icon (to the left of the uninstall bin) and delete the game data. This will erase your save progress, but it should allow the game to launch without crashing. 

Love the game-Never play Civ before and there is so much offered in game play that I look forward to playing and learning with new strategies each time. Amazing job developers.

Old Review - Played for a couple of days and on the 3rd day the game won't load. The title screen shows, then the trademark screen, and then crashes and brings me back to my Meta games page. I've uninstalled and re-installed twice and same point of crash every time.

First experience play table was going everywhere, game just isn’t stable now it’s not working at all every time I try to play it gets to loading screen the kicks me out to meta main screen. I have hard re booted headset twice this didn’t work I have now completely wiped the headset back to factory settings reinstalled the game and it still throws me out as soon as it gets to loading screens worst experience I have ever had since owning a quest. What a disappointment considering I loved playing this gameyears ago and jumped to get it when I seen it in the store.

I have played civilization from civilization 1 in  91 ,I am a big fan .I was excited to purchase this game ,and I did ,but it's awful .It's more like a Bata version than a new game.I would stay away from this one .I tried to return but went over my 2 hour play ,warning if you do the tutorial it will burn that time quickly as you are going back due to freezing g and crashes. I wanted to enjoy this but until they fix it and make it playable it's a joke or worse yet a money grab .

I dont get what all the hate is about. I love this game, I've played prolly 20 hours in the couple days I've owned it. It's a dungeon crawler really close to dnd. If you like table top turn based RPG. This is right up your alley

reviews.review_title: Read the reviews still fell for it
reviews.review_description:
Unfortunately, I read the reviews warning me not to buy this and they came true, got an hour or so of play time, really enjoyed it and was looking forward to playing again and it just crashes right at the title screen.

I really wanted this to work, it’s so disappointing. Hopeful for a fix patch at some point

1. The user interface is lacking in info the pc version has or it is not easy to display would like to see this tweaked.
2. The render on some units and other assets tends to bug out or not show properly examples units going bald or when they fight not show weapons or arrows.
3.the game has no shadows at all im not sure if this can be fixed but its odd to not see a single shadow cast by anythimg,
4. I assume this is just a civ 7 thing but having enemy civs start literally 6 or 7 tiles from you is very annoying and makes no sense.
5.Zooming out is an issue its hard to read the unit icons from a distance and with only being able to see so many tiles at at time its hard to track enemy movement all over the map. since you cant really watch them it should pan camera to enemy moves in your line of sight at least to give you info on whats happening around u in between turns

reviews.review_title: Looks great, may not be for everyone
reviews.review_description: My problem with these games has always been my inability to deal with all the different things to keep in order.  I'd hoped VR would change that, but unfortunately it's as complicated  as I recall. If you can focus long enough and stay engaged through the tutorial, this is for you, but as another reviewer mentioned...it's not for casual gamers.   I believe that it is going to be an amazing game, but I started to get bored and annoyed by the lack of knowing what to do next, despite still being in the tutorial.  I'm sure there's a lot baked in that I still haven't come across, but at it's current price point, I'm not willing to stick around any longer than I need to before the refund window closes.  I just know I won't play it enough to justify keeping it.  I do recommend it though if these types of strategy games are your cup of tea.

When I was a young teenager, I discovered Civ4. To me, it was like playing Risk, on a computer, with far deeper control over what was happening. I was glued to my computer for the first time.

That was the first, and last Civ I played. Computer and flat screen games in general slowly started to have me losing interest due to the wall between the player, and the action.

When I heard about Civ7 coming to Quest, I really hoped it would feel like playing a very deep board game. When I saw the early reviews, I was scared that it would not meet my expectations... Given the very forgiving refund program from Meta, I decided to try it myself, and have my own opinion on the game.

It is exactly what I was hoping for and more.

Civ7 plays very smoothly - for me - on the Meta Quest 3. I don't have FPS issues and I play exclusively in mixed reality. The game allows me to drop it on a table near me, sit and watch my surroundings while conquering the world. The depth of the game is unmatched by any other game on the platform and it is definitely worth its price.

Great job to the team, thank you for the port and for making the VR library more fleshed out.

The team achieved something I never thought would be possible, a full Civilization release for VR. I bought it first for the novelty of it but I quickly fell in love with this version of the game. Yes, there are definitely some visual hiccups such as some of the 3D models popping in and UI elements overlapping in undesirable ways but this is through and through a Civilization game. I have been lucky enough not to have any of the stuttering problems I'm reading from others, the game runs quite smoothly for me. I've been playing the game for hours each day, it's currently the only game I own that I can sit and play in VR so it's a pretty relaxing experience. I hope the team can iron out the bugs for others so everyone can get the same experience I've been getting out of it.

Update: After spending more time with the game unfortunately I have to downgrade it a star. There are certain problems I've come across that have really hurt my experience. After playing for a while I have noticed the models for units will disappear, the UI tab for promoting units will not work sometimes, cities are hard to differentiate from the worker tiles so selecting them directly is hard, City border will sometimes disappear and once my cloud save got corrupted causing me to have to delete my save files to regain access to the entire game. Most of these can be fixed by reloading a save game but they happen so often and reloading takes so long that it seriously hurts my enjoyment of the game.

I began to enjoy the game until after my second gaming session, it crashed and will not restart. I have tried deleting, reinstalling multiple times, and restarting my headset to no avail. I waited 24 hours to try again and still has the same problem.

reviews.review_title: Not deserving of the Sid Seal
reviews.review_description: Lots of glitches, slow and cumbersome play with limited to no assistance or training. Nothing really makes intuitive sense, and the graphics are worse than the floppy disc version.  Don't waste your money!

I like the game, we'll get that out of the way.  I wish a lot.  I wish the graphic quality was better.  I wish all menus were available, I wish I could tell what year it is.  I wish I could get info like a unit list etc.  all stuff available to PC, and now they got a patch that allows them to continue playing beyond the sudden and unexpected end.  In reality, this should have been PCVR through steam, sorry to say.  The computing power of the headset is just not there for a game of this complexity.
Bad just bad I played for 30 minutes and just hated everything the UI is bad the gameplay is bad and building a city is bad. Don't buy it

What do you call it when you sell someone a product, but the product doesn’t work and you refuse to refund the costumer? Theft. Ever since I got the metaquest 3 I have been eagerly purchasing games and apps from them. You would imagine that they would have the decency to refund me when the product constantly crashes and does not work as intended.  Do they have a phone number where I can explain the situation to a human? Of course not. How convenient. Meta is just another powerful money-grabbing corporation that arbitrarily determines they get to keep my money when a game they sold me forever stops working after the first day of use. I regret having ever trusted them with my business. Honestly, I wish I could just get a refund for everything I have ever bought from them, including the goggles, and never have to use their products again.  Instead, the system rewards them for stealing. How do I get in contact with someone or appeal the decision? Impossible, I just have to accept that they steal my money. I despise their modus operandi and wish them the worst.

I experienced countless bugs in my first hours of gameplay.

All Voice lines were not working, graphical bugs in the units, not being able to scroll through instructions, zooming in and out is way too touchy, wartable specifically is buggy.

For comfort, its not comfortable to play sitting or laying as the wrist must bend down to point at the floor. The game table was impossible to move around. I like the UI.

I liked using the grip to move the camera, but it needs to be more sensitive, or a way to change the sensitivity.
The app ran normally at first, but now it crashes at start.  I have re-download the app, and factory reset my headset.  Nothing worked.  I am hoping in an update this will be fixed.

This will be a living review as I make my way through the game.  I wanted to update real fast to confirm with the latest patch released on 4/10 that the stuttering issues at the war table have been resolved AND performance is super fluid.   (Thank you devs for addressing the issue so quickly!).  

Love the Museum and the grand view of the ancient city in the war table room. Controls seem to be easy enough to pick up and get used to. The graphics while not on par with the PC version of the game look fine to me considering this is a stand alone quest version in VR with a lot of performance considerations to be made.  

Seems like a authentic Civ 7 experience so far and now it's time for me to sit back, play and enjoy the game.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for your feedback and sorry you’re experiencing some performance issues. Please download the most recent update for Civilization VII - VR that has just been deployed this morning. This patch should help fix some of the performance issues you've experienced. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: Would be a 5 if not for the crashes
reviews.review_description:
I've sunk a ton of hours in III, IV, V and VI. I held off getting VII when it released on flat because I only pick it up when decent updates have been pushed into it.

But I'm a big VR fan and a Civ fan, so this was an instant buy.

This will be a time killer for me, but the only thing stopping me from playing until 3 in the morning is the frequent crashes.

Fix that, and this thing is a winner.

IDGAF what you all say. I got GOOSE bumps on just the intro. I am still learning how to maneuver but this is EVERYTHING and more than what I expected. So for those trashing the game, don’t listen to them. I plan to lose myself in this game. Awesome job!

I’m not sure if my review will be of any help as I come with no prior civilization experience. However, I can only describe my experience of the game so far as like seeing those epic action packed video ads for a mobile game. But when you actually play the game, it in reality is just a very basic pixel ladder or turn based game. The intro video was so empowering and exciting but when I get into the actual gameplay interaction, I am left at a loss as to what I am supposed to do. It does a great job of teaching the controls but I am otherwise left clueless and just clicking random things. Perhaps this game is meant more for someone that has played civilization before. Till my patience runs out, I’ll keep giving this game a try but unfortunately I am so far considering refunding this game that could be better allocated to other games I’ve been wanting to purchase. If you’d like to see my gameplay so far, I’ll be uploading on youtube.

reviews.review_title: Multiplayer Not working in so many ways
reviews.review_description: Developers.. the multiplayere has so many bugs... is there a way we can contribute with bug fixing

The game was good for the time I was able to play it.

However I think they messed up on the file upload. And gave us the demo version.
After 2 or 4 hours of playing i can not launch game again. 
Keeps crashing on intro page.
Waited a week and back again to try and still not fixed.

Buy at own risk - I will update review when working again.

————————————————————

“Edit” Found a fix thanks to @bodkins

On your Quest go into Storage > Click on Civ 7 and clear cache. Game should start working.
However keep in mind this also clears you save file. 😞

reviews.review_title: Nice but quite a few issues.
reviews.review_description: I played it for 30 mins, now it doesn't load anymore. Will wait for a fix from the dev. I guess.. EDIT - I had to figure out myself how to make it load again, no words from the developer, I do not recommend buying this game right now.
Has many glitches - frustrating!
At $60 it feels like a bit of a rip off. It's buggy. Missing assets from the 2d version. I want this to be better

Have tried multiple times to get code to sign up for 2k account and I'm never sent a code to finish the process. Over now tried numerous times and still no code. It's not in my junk file either. Only purchased the game to play multiplayer.
